I read the problem statement and prior-turn log. The prompt‚Äôs required syntax is the four-generator tame format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and constants reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log convincingly kills the early candidates in Turns 3, 6, and 8; the only serious surviving word is the Turn 9‚Äì22 dynamic tame-inertia candidate.

The live candidate is still variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with œÄ=œÄ2 and Œ∑=2œÄ,r=xœÉ‚àíœÄxœÉœÄx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ ((yœÑ)œÄ)œÉy‚àí1,where œÄ‚â°40491355905,‚àíœÄ‚â°45176306175,‚àíŒ∑=‚àí2œÄ ‚â°50932630783(modM).I did not find a new finite-tame counterexample. My new contribution is to sharpen Turn 22‚Äôs ‚Äúexact Frobenius action‚Äù calculation. Turn 22 treated unramified quotients of degree f=2a, where œÄ‚â°1(modf), so œÉœÄ can literally be replaced by œÉ. That is correct only in the pure 2-power unramified case. For general unramified degreef=2a m,m¬Ýodd,the exact filtered action contains an extra 2-adic unit power of S=œÉf. This term is invisible in the mod-2 Frattini calculation but is visible in higher filtration layers, precisely where the remaining formation gap lives.Lets=sf ‚â°œÄ(modf),so s‚â°1(mod2a),s‚â°0(modm).In the pro-2 kernel above the unramified degree-f quotient, defineq=qf=f1‚àís‚ààZ2.

Then, in the pro-2 kernel,œÉœÄ=œÉsSq,S=œÉf.

This is the datum missing from the pure 2a -formula. Examples:f1234612 s=œÄmodf010139 q=(1‚àís)/f101/30‚àí1/3‚àí2/3 Thus f=4 will not see the new phenomenon; f=3,6,12,‚Ä¶ do.Now specialize the live candidate to œÑ=1. The relation becomesr=xœÉ‚àíœÄxœÉœÄ [œÉ,y].For the unramified quotient of degree f, choose representatives 1,œÉ,‚Ä¶,œÉ f‚àí1, and putxi=œÉixœÉ‚àíi,yi=œÉiyœÉ‚àíi,S=œÉf.Let indices i‚àís and i+1 be reduced modulo f. DefineŒΩi={0,‚àí1,i‚â•s,i<s,œµi={1,0,i=f‚àí1,iÓÄÝ=f‚àí1.

Then the exact Reidemeister‚ÄìSchreier relators areri=xiSŒΩi‚àíqxi‚àísS q‚àíŒΩiSœµiyi+1S‚àíœµiyi‚àí1,i=0,‚Ä¶,f‚àí1.This is the general unramified exact normal form selected by the live candidate. For f=2a, s=1 and q=0, so it reduces to Turn 22‚Äôs formulari=xixi‚àí1yi+1yi‚àí1 with only the usual boundary S-corrections. For f=1, it gives the base dyadic Demu≈°kin-looking relationxS‚àí1 xS[S,y],i.e. the corrected xz‚àí1 xz[z,y] relation from the prior turns.

The mod-2 linearization of the exact relators isXi+Xi‚àís+Yi+1+Yi=0,or, in A=F2 [T]/(Tf‚àí1),(1+T‚àís)X+(1+T)Y=0.This recovers the expected Frattini rank f+2. If f is even, s is odd, so 1+T‚à£1+T‚àís,and the correct elimination is

Y=Q(T)X+YÀâ,Q(T)=1+T1+T‚àís,withYÀâ the invariant Y-class. This is the direction of the quotient that must be used in any final proof. With this Q, the reduced multiplier is h(T)=1+Q(T)+Q(T‚àí1).

For a nontrivial f-th root Œ∂,(1+Œ∂)h(Œ∂)=Œ∂‚àís+Œ∂s+1.

So h(Œ∂)=0 would forceŒ∂2s+1=1.But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), hence gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Thus the trivial unramified block is nonsingular, agreeing with the prior cup-product conclusion, but now with the exact Sq -terms accounted for.Conceptually, the live word is best read using the pro-2 Frobenius componentœÜ=œÉœÄ.ThenœÑœÜ=œÑ2œÄ=œÑŒ∑,and the relation can be rewritten asr=xœÜ‚àí1xœÜx‚àí1(x(œÑœÜ)‚àí1)œÄ ((yœÑ)œÄ)œÉy‚àí1.

This explains why the fixed œÑ‚àí2 correction from Turn 7 failed at (e,f)=(91,12): it used œÑ‚àíœÉ instead of œÑ‚àíœÉœÄ. The dynamic exponent is not an arbitrary patch; it is exactly the tame-inertia action of the pro-2 Frobenius component.The next arithmetic comparison should therefore not start with f=4, as Turn 22 suggested, because qf=0 there and the new term is invisible. A better test is f=3 or f=6, whereœÉœÄ=S1/3orœÉœÄ=œÉ3S‚àí1/3,respectively. Those cases probe the higher-filtered Sq -part of the action while still being much smaller than mixed tame cases such as (91,12).

ESTABLISHED: The live Turn 9‚Äì22 candidate remains unrefuted. I derived the exact unramified kernel relators for arbitrary unramified degree f, including the essential term œÉœÄ=œÉsSq with q=(1‚àís)/f‚ààZ2. Turn 22‚Äôs exact formula is the special case f=2a, where q=0. The dynamic œÑ‚àí2œÄ correction is conceptually (œÑœÉœÄ)‚àí1, i.e. it is forced by the pro-2 Frobenius component.

GAP: This still does not prove the presentation. The remaining gap is the same dyadic formation/rigidity problem, now sharpened: the exact filtered arithmetic Frobenius action must be matched with the candidate‚Äôs action including the Sq -terms for unramified degrees with odd part, not only the pure 2-power cases or low Zassenhaus shadows.

NEXT: Compare the exact f=3 and f=6 candidate actions with arithmetic Kummer/Witt data over the corresponding unramified extensions. These cases should detect the S1/3 and S‚àí1/3 terms that are invisible in the f=4 test.[BREAKTHROUGH]
